description:
Goal: World::new should allocate stable runtime identities and namespace entries without lexing or parsing runtime source. Signal: the Compiler2 quicksort trace-summary reports zero pre-drive lexer/parser work, while quicksort and Enum.reduce still pull referenced runtime modules through ordinary Compiler2 jobs. Strategy: after activation/fact ownership is corrected, turn runtime bootstrap into a reference-only surface and let runtime source enter the same Code/Index/Scope/Define path as user source when demanded.

Change:
- Remove eager runtime lex/parse from Compiler2 construction.
- Bootstrap stable runtime module/function references and namespace bindings only.
- Pull runtime source, protocol callbacks, imports, and impl surfaces through ordinary Compiler2 jobs on first demand.

Why:
- Runtime work before the first drive violates the pull-based model and hides real compile cost outside the work graph.

Checks:
- Quicksort trace-summary reports zero pre-drive frontend work.
- Runtime-backed quicksort and Enum.reduce scenarios still settle through ordinary Compiler2 jobs.
title: Compiler2 runtime bootstrap: replace eager parsing with lazy surface bootstrap
